seo.metaTitle: 7-Day Yunnan Endemic Birding — Lashi, Laojun & Baihualing
seo.metaDescription: A focused small-group birding tour of northwest Yunnan. Three ecosystems, one critically-endemic bird (Biet's Laughingthrush), and the Laojun Mountain birding station. 7 days, 200-300 species target, IUCN-aligned.

sections.subtitle: Three ecosystems, one critically-endemic bird
sections.text: A focused 7-day loop through northwest Yunnan's three most distinct birding ecosystems — Lashi highland wetland, Laojun Mountain mid-elevation oak-rhododendron forest, and Baihualing sub-tropical valleys. The headline target is the Biet's Laughingthrush (Garrulax bieti), found only here and reliably seen at the Laojun Mountain birding station we partner with. Small group (2-4 guests), expert local guides, 200-300 species target.

items.title: Headline Target
items.subtitle: Biet's Laughingthrush
items.highlight: Critically endangered, found only in NW Yunnan, reliably seen at the Laojun Mountain birding station

items.title: Duration
items.subtitle: Seven Days
items.highlight: Day 1: 10 AM to 9:30 PM | Days 2-7: 5:30 AM to 9:00 PM

items.title: Group Size
items.subtitle: 2-4 Guests
items.highlight: Pre-tripped Swarovski optics (8x42 binos + scope) included

items.title: Biet's Laughingthrush Anchor Day
items.subtitle: Laojun Mountain birding station
items.text: Two full mornings at the Laojun Mountain birding station with a local guide who can reliably bring the critically-endemic Biet's Laughingthrush into view. The species is restricted to NW Yunnan and has not been recorded in significant numbers anywhere else.

items.title: Three-Ecosystem Loop
items.subtitle: Lashi → Laojun → Baihualing
items.text: A clean three-stop linear arc through three distinct ecosystems — highland wetland, mid-elevation montane forest, and sub-tropical valley — with no internal flights. Total driving ~14-15 hours across the week, all between 25 min and 5-6 hours.

items.title: IUCN-Aligned Operation
items.subtitle: Lukasz is IUCN-CEC Asia awardee
items.text: Cloud Mountain's lead coordinator holds the 2024 IUCN-CEC Regional Award for Asia. Trip design follows IUCN sustainable-tourism guidelines: pre-tripped optics, locally-owned homestays, payments to the Laojun and Baihualing birding stations, and no factory-shop commissions.

items.title: 200-300 Species Target
items.subtitle: Trip-list printed and eBird-uploaded
items.text: Realistic species target across the week. The lead guide prints a one-page eBird-formatted trip list and uploads your records so your sightings stay with you long after the trip ends. Most guests pick up 50-100+ lifers depending on prior experience.

items.title: Laojun Mountain Birding Station
items.subtitle: Lisu village team
items.text: Two full mornings at the Laojun Mountain birding station, partnered with the local Lisu-minority team who maintain the trail network, run call playback, and know exactly where Biet's Laughingthrush moves through the oak-rhododendron forest. Your stay directly funds the station.

items.title: Baihualing Sub-Tropical Valleys
items.subtitle: Hou Tiguo's team
items.text: Two full days at Baihualing, partnered with Hou Tiguo's experienced local team. Sub-tropical elevation (1,400-2,600m) gives a completely different species mix from Lashi and Laojun - Red-tailed Laughingthrush, Silver-eared Mesia, and the dense mixed-flocks Hou Tiguo is famous for finding.

items.title: Pre-Tripped Swarovski Optics
items.subtitle: 8x42 binos + scope per group
items.text: We pre-trip 1 Swarovski 8x42 binocular per guest + 1 shared Swarovski spotting scope for the group. Most serious birders bring their own; ours are an excellent fallback. No need to lug gear through Kunming Airport.

items.title: Lashi Crane Flight at Dusk
items.subtitle: Common Crane returning to roost
items.text: Our Day 1 evening is timed to the cranes: as the sun sets over Lashi Wetland, Common Crane and Black Stork return to their roost on the lake. Golden hour light, 15-20 minutes of pure spectacle, before the welcome dinner.

image.altText: Common Crane in flight at sunset

items.title: Small Group, Deep Focus
items.subtitle: 6-8 hours daily through bins
items.text: Strict cap of 2-4 guests. This is not a group tour. The lead guide can spend 6-8 hours a day looking through binoculars with each guest, sharing scope time, and quietly pointing out the birds you'd otherwise miss. The small group is the whole point.

image.altText: Small group birding at a wetland

items.title: Conservation-First Pricing
items.subtitle: Direct payments to birding stations
items.text: Trip fees include direct payments to the Laojun Mountain birding station and to Hou Tiguo's Baihualing team. Cloud Mountain's own margin is on the order of 2.0-2.2x - tighter than typical eco-tour markups - so more of your fee goes to the people and places doing the conservation work.

items.title: Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey Possibility
items.subtitle: Baima Snow Mountain
items.text: Guests with an extra day before or after the tour can add a side-trip to Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve to see the Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey in the wild. Fewer than 3,000 individuals in the world; observed from a distance with the local conservation rangers.

image.altText: Yunnan Snub-nosed Monkey in natural habitat

items.title: Authentic Local Cuisine
items.subtitle: No tourist-trap restaurants
items.text: All meals are at locally-owned restaurants. Naxi courtyard breakfasts in Lijiang, Lisu village homestay dinners at Laojun, Tibetan momo in Shangri-La. We do not take guests to commission-paying shops or tourist-trap restaurants. Dietary requirements (vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free) are easy to accommodate.

days.title: Arrival + Lashi Conservation Context + First Birding

activities: Pickup at Lijiang Sanyi Airport (LJG) or your Old Town hotel (10 AM-noon arrivals), 30-min transfer to a boutique Naxi courtyard in Lijiang Old Town - check-in, light brunch, settle in. After lunch, 25-min drive to Lashi Wetland Park for the conservation context session at the Lashi Bird Museum and Conservation Bureau, hosted by the local conservation biologist who manages the wetland's crane wintering program. Pre-dusk birding on the Lashi causeways - the second-best window of the day - Common Crane and Black Stork flight back to their roost at sunset, golden-hour light. Welcome dinner at a Naxi courtyard restaurant in Sanyuan village, trip briefing and checklist primer with the lead guide, and an optional 30-45 min owl listening session along the wetland edge (Collared Scops Owl near-guaranteed, Mountain Scops Owl possible).

days.title: Lashi Dawn Anchor + Transfer to Laojun Mountain

activities: 5:30 AM out with the local guide - dawn at Lashi is the anchor wetland session, cranes leave roost at first light, waterfowl flights, passerines in the reedbeds. This is the productive wetland window of the entire trip. 9:00 AM brunch and checklist review, then 9:30 AM drive Lijiang to Laojun Mountain (1.5 hr, elevation 2,400m to ~2,800m). 11:00 AM lunch at Li Ming village, a Lisu-minority village in the Laojun foothills, followed by an approach walk to the birding station - ~30 min into mid-elevation oak-rhododendron forest. First light Biet's Laughingthrush (Garrulax bieti) possible if quiet. Village homestay dinner and briefing on the Biet's Laughingthrush plan for tomorrow.

days.title: Biet's Laughingthrush Anchor Day at Laojun Station

activities: 5:30 AM coffee + biscuits, out with the local Laojun station guide. 6:00-11:00 AM full morning at the station - Biet's Laughingthrush is the headline, the local guide knows the call playback and positioning that brings it in. 11:00 AM-3:00 PM lunch break at the village homestay, nap, lead guide walks guests through the morning's eBird checklist. 3:00-6:00 PM afternoon session on a lower-elevation trail for different species - Golden Bush Robin, White-browed Shortwing, and mixed flocks of fulvettas and sibias. Dinner with the Laojun Lisu village elder - he tells the story of how the birding station started.

days.title: Laojun Second Morning + Transfer to Baihualing

activities: 5:30-10:00 AM final morning at the Laojun station - a different micro-habitat walk today, chase species missed on Day 3. 10:30 AM drive Laojun to Baihualing via the Hangrui Expressway (5-6 hours), lunch stop at a highway town. Afternoon drive drops through the Salween (Nujiang) river gorge - optional stop at the Nu River overlook for raptors. Late afternoon arrival at Baihualing village, check into a village homestay, meet Hou Tiguo's team. Briefing on Baihualing's 520+ species and dinner at the homestay with a local Lisu-style meal.

5:30 AM out with Hou Tiguo's team for the birding station - the famous Baihualing hide setup. Baihualing is lower elevation, hotter, more humid - different species mix from Laojun. Targets: Red-tailed Laughingthrush, Rusty-capped Fulvetta, Silver-eared Mesia, Red-billed Leiothrix, plus mixed-flocks of warblers and flycatchers. 3:00-5:00 PM different trail - the Laifengshan approach for Silver Pheasant and mixed-flocks of laughingthrushes, bird checklist and eBird upload with the lead guide. Village homestay dinner.

days.title: Baihualing Second Day + Return Drive to Lijiang

activities: 5:30-10:00 AM final Baihualing morning - local guides know which micro-site is best for the species we missed on Day 5. 10:30 AM drive back to Lijiang (5-6 hours), lunch stop at Yongping - clean highway-town restaurant. Late afternoon arrival in Lijiang Old Town, check into a boutique hotel. Farewell dinner at a Naxi courtyard restaurant, trip list review - the lead guide prints a one-page eBird trip-list for each guest.

days.title: Lijiang Old Town Cultural Close + Departure

activities: Optional Naxi Old Town walk for guests who want one last cultural experience, coffee at a Lijiang Old Town cafe. Then transfer to Lijiang Sanyi Airport (LJG) for departure flights - or, optional half-day add-on: drive to Lashi for one final wetland morning if guests have a late flight. Trip end.

contactNote.text: For single-guest bookings or groups of 5+

included.text: Lead Coordinator (Lukasz Madrzynski, IUCN-CEC Asia awardee), Local Birding Guides (Laojun station + Hou Tiguo at Baihualing), 1 Swarovski 8x42 binocular per guest + 1 shared spotting scope, All Accommodation (4-star equivalent or village homestay), All Meals (B/L/D), Private Transportation, All Park + Reserve Entrance Fees, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: International Flights to/from Lijiang, Personal Expenses, Voluntary Gratuities
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 10 AM at Lijiang Sanyi Airport (LJG) or your Lijiang Old Town hotel | Return: ~6 PM at Lijiang Sanyi Airport

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: What is the success rate for Biet's Laughingthrush?
items.answer: Near-100% on Day 3 at the Laojun station. The local guide uses call playback and the species is reliably seen in a 4-5 hour morning session. We've not had a miss in our reconnaisance trips.

items.question: What optics are provided?
items.answer: We pre-trip 1 Swarovski 8x42 binocular per guest + 1 shared Swarovski spotting scope for the group. You're welcome to bring your own if you have preferences, but most guests use ours and find them excellent.

items.question: Is this suitable for serious birders only?
items.answer: It's designed for serious birders - people who have already done Costa Rica, East Africa, or similar trips. If you're a casual birder expecting a relaxed nature experience, our regular Lashi Wetland half-day or the Shangri-La Monkey Trip are better fits.

items.question: How high do we go and how is altitude handled?
items.answer: Laojun Mountain station sits at 2,800m with day-walks up to 3,500m. Baihualing ranges 1,400-2,600m. The itinerary is built with gradual acclimatization (Lashi 2,440m on Day 1, then progression to higher elevations). Most guests adjust comfortably in 24-48 hours.

items.question: Can the trip be extended to 8 days?
items.answer: Yes - the 8-day version adds a second day at either Laojun or Baihualing. We recommend the extra day at Baihualing because it has 5x the species count of Laojun and weather buffer. Surcharge: ~$600/person.

items.question: What's the cancellation policy?
items.answer: Full refund if cancelled 90+ days before departure. 75% refund for 60-89 days. 50% refund for 30-59 days. No refund within 30 days. We strongly recommend travel insurance.

items.question: When is the best time of year?
items.answer: March-May and October-November are the two prime windows. Spring (Apr-May) is best for passerine migration and breeding plumage. Autumn (Oct-Nov) is best for raptors and winter visitors. Summer is rainy; winter is colder but still productive.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation?
items.answer: Cloud Mountain is an IUCN member. Trip fees include payments to the Laojun Mountain birding station and Hou Tiguo's Baihualing team. We do not take guests to commission-paying shops, and we contribute trip-list data to local conservation bureaus for monitoring purposes.
